member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.DefaultMessageRouter.GetEndpointsForBroadcast(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServiceEndpoint})
member.summary: Broadcast to all available endpoints including online and offline, when the endpoint is offline, there is a need to notify user about it because there is potential message loss

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.DefaultMessageRouter.GetEndpointsForUser(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServiceEndpoint})
member.summary: Broadcast to all available endpoints including online and offline, when the endpoint is offline, there is a need to notify user about it because there is potential message loss

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.DefaultMessageRouter.GetEndpointsForGroup(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServiceEndpoint})
member.summary: Broadcast to all available endpoints including online and offline, when the endpoint is offline, there is a need to notify user about it because there is potential message loss

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.DefaultMessageRouter.GetEndpointsForConnection(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServiceEndpoint})
member.summary: Broadcast to all available endpoints including online and offline, when the endpoint is offline, there is a need to notify user about it because there is potential message loss Note that this one is only called when the SDK is not able to identify where the connectionId is. When the outcoming connectionId happens to be also connected to this app server, SDK can directly send the messages back to that connectionId

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.IMessageRouter.GetEndpointsForBroadcast(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServiceEndpoint})
member.summary: Get the service endpoints for broadcast message to send to

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.IMessageRouter.GetEndpointsForUser(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServiceEndpoint})
member.summary: Get the service endpoints for the specified user to send to

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.IMessageRouter.GetEndpointsForGroup(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServiceEndpoint})
member.summary: Get the service endpoints for the specified group to send to

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.IMessageRouter.GetEndpointsForConnection(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServiceEndpoint})
member.summary: Get the service endpoints for the specified connection to send to Note that this one is only called when the SDK is not able to identify where the connectionId is. When the outcoming connectionId happens to be also connected to this app server, SDK can directly send the messages back to that connectionId

member.name: T:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServerStickyMode
member.summary: Specifies the mode for server sticky, when client is always routed to the server which it first /negotiate with, we call it "server sticky mode".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServerStickyMode.Disabled
member.summary: We the server sticky mode is disabled, it picks the server connection by some algorithm In general, local server connection first least client connections routed server connection first

member.name: F:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServerStickyMode.Required
member.summary: We will try to find the server it /negotiate with from both local and global route table, it the server is not connected, throw, If it is globally routed, this request will be always globally routed

member.name: P:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServiceEndpoint.Online
member.summary: Initial status as Online so that when the app server first starts, it can accept incoming negotiate requests, as for backward compatability

member.name: T:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.Common.AzureSignalRAccessTokenTooLongException
member.summary: The exception thrown when AccessToken is too long.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.Common.AzureSignalRAccessTokenTooLongException.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.Common.AzureSignalRAccessTokenTooLongException.#ctor(System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationInfo,System.Runtime.Serialization.StreamingContext)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The that holds the serialized object data about the exception being thrown.
The that contains contextual information about the source or destination.
member.exception: The class name is null or is zero (0).

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

The that contains contextual information about the source or destination.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.Common.ServiceConnectionNotActiveException.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.Common.BackOffPolicy.CallProbeWithBackOffAsync(System.Func{System.Threading.Tasks.Task{System.Boolean}},System.Func{System.Int32,System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Provides a synchronized mechanism of calling probing funcs by multiple concurrent callers. Each caller's probe func will be invoked exactly one time. The probe call may get delayed depending on the result of previous probe calls. The delay is controlled by getRetryDelay func and the number of consecutive failed probe calls.
member.param: this func returns a task with boolean result indicating if the probe was successful
this func returns a TimeSpan delay for a given iteration number
member.returns: A task with its final state and result matching the state and result of the task returned by the probe func. This task will completes after either of the following happens: - probe's task changes state to task.IsCompletedSuccessfully == true and task.Result == true - probe's task.Result == false / faulted / cancelled and the delay defined by getRetryDelay has passed

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ConnectionFactory.GracefulLoggerFactory.GracefulLogger.Log``1(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.EventId,``0,System.Exception,System.Func{``0,System.Exception,System.String})
member.summary: Downgrade error level logs, and also exclude exception details Exceptions thrown from inside the HttpConnection are supposed to be handled by the caller and logged with more user-friendly message

member.name: F:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServerConnectionType.Default
member.summary: 0, Default, it can carry clients, service runtime should always accept this kind of connection

member.name: F:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServerConnectionType.OnDemand
member.summary: 1, OnDemand, creating when service requested more connections, it can carry clients, but it may be rejected by service runtime.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServerConnectionType.Weak
member.summary: 2, Weak, it can not carry clients, but it can send message

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServiceConnectionBase.StartAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Start a service connection without the lifetime management. To get full lifetime management including dispose or restart, use

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServiceConnectionContainerBase.StartCoreAsync(Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.IServiceConnection,System.String)
member.summary: Start and manage the whole connection lifetime

member.name: M:Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServiceConnectionContainerBase.CreateServiceConnectionCore(Microsoft.Azure.SignalR.ServerConnectionType)
member.summary: Create a connection for a specific service connection type
